seo_title: 16 New Startups Join MIT's STARTnano Program to Develop Hard-Tech…
meta_description: MIT's STARTnano program has announced 16 new startups developing hard-tech solutions for health, climate, energy, and more. Learn how these startups are…
body_html:
Sixteen new startups have joined MIT's STARTnano program to develop hard-tech solutions with the support of MIT.nano.

The STARTnano program supports early-stage companies from MIT and beyond with the tools and network they need for success. The program aims to increase the survival rate of hard-tech startups by easing their journey from the lab to the real world. Program participants receive access to MIT.nano's laboratories, as well as opportunities to present at startup exhibits at MIT conferences and exclusive events.

Developing Solutions for Global Challenges

Health The newly engaged startups are developing solutions to address some of the world's greatest health challenges.

Climate The startups are also working on climate-related projects, including renewable energy and sustainable materials.

Energy Solutions for energy efficiency and sustainability are being developed by the new cohort of startups.

Semiconductors The startups are working on innovative semiconductor solutions to support the development of emerging technologies.

Novel Materials New materials and manufacturing techniques are being developed by the new cohort of startups.

Quantum Computing The startups are working on quantum computing projects, including superconductor discovery and development.

Expert Insights “The unique resources of MIT.nano enable not just the foundational research of academia, but the translation of that research into commercial innovations through startups,” says STARTnano Program Manager Joyce Wu SM ’00, PhD ’07.

Discounted use of MIT.nano shared facilities Guided access to the MIT innovation ecosystem Opportunities to present at startup exhibits at MIT conferences and exclusive events

source.title: Sixteen new START.nano companies are developing hard-tech solutions with the support of MIT.nano
source.summary: Startup accelerator program grows to over 30 companies, almost half of them with MIT pedigrees.
